Detailed Description

Wrapper for PGP/SMIME calls to GPGME.

static void redraw_if_needed ( gpgme_ctx_t  ctx)
static

accommodate for a redraw if needed

Parameters
ctxGPGME handle

Definition at line 202 of file crypt_gpgme.c.

203 {
204 #if (GPGME_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x010800)
205  /* gpgme_get_ctx_flag is not available in GPGME < 1.8.0. In this case, stay
206  * on the safe side and always redraw. */
207  (void) ctx;
208  mutt_need_hard_redraw();
209 #else
210  const char *s = gpgme_get_ctx_flag(ctx, "redraw");
211  if (!s /* flag not known */ || *s /* flag true */)
212  {
213  mutt_need_hard_redraw();
214  }
215 #endif
216 }

1004  goto cleanup;

1013  {
1014  if (fwrite(buf, nread, 1, fp) != 1)
1015  {
1016  mutt_perror(mutt_b2s(tempf));

1018  unlink(mutt_b2s(tempf));

1020  }
1021  }
1022  }

1026  mutt_file_fclose(&fp);

1030  unlink(mutt_b2s(tempf));
1031  mutt_file_fclose(&fp);
1032  goto cleanup;
1033  }
1034  if (fp_ret)
1035  *fp_ret = fp;
1036  rv = mutt_buffer_strdup(tempf);
1037 
1038 cleanup:
1039  mutt_buffer_pool_release(&tempf);
1040  return rv;

1182  {

1485  * unset and there is no default key specified in ~/.gnupg/gpg.conf */
1486  sigres = gpgme_op_sign_result(ctx);
1487  if (!sigres->signatures)
1488  {
1489  gpgme_data_release(signature);
1490  gpgme_release(ctx);
1491  mutt_error(_("$pgp_sign_as unset and no default key specified in "
1492  "~/.gnupg/gpg.conf"));
1493  return NULL;
1494  }
1495 
1496  sigfile = data_object_to_tempfile(signature, NULL);
1497  gpgme_data_release(signature);
1498  if (!sigfile)
1499  {
1500  gpgme_release(ctx);
1501  return NULL;
1502  }
1503 
1504  t = mutt_body_new();
1505  t->type = TYPE_MULTIPART;
1506  t->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("signed");
1507  t->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1508  t->use_disp = false;
1509  t->disposition = DISP_INLINE;
1510 
1511  mutt_generate_boundary(&t->parameter);
1512  mutt_param_set(&t->parameter, "protocol",
1513  use_smime ? "application/pkcs7-signature" : "application/pgp-signature");
1514  /* Get the micalg from GPGME. Old gpgme versions don't support this
1515  * for S/MIME so we assume sha-1 in this case. */
1516  if (get_micalg(ctx, use_smime, buf, sizeof(buf)) == 0)
1517  mutt_param_set(&t->parameter, "micalg", buf);
1518  else if (use_smime)
1519  mutt_param_set(&t->parameter, "micalg", "sha1");
1520  gpgme_release(ctx);
1521 
1522  t->parts = a;
1523  a = t;
1524 
1525  t->parts->next = mutt_body_new();
1526  t = t->parts->next;
1527  t->type = TYPE_APPLICATION;
1528  if (use_smime)
1529  {
1530  t->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("pkcs7-signature");
1531  mutt_param_set(&t->parameter, "name", "smime.p7s");
1532  t->encoding = ENC_BASE64;
1533  t->use_disp = true;
1534  t->disposition = DISP_ATTACH;
1535  t->d_filename = mutt_str_strdup("smime.p7s");
1536  }
1537  else
1538  {
1539  t->subtype = mutt_str_strdup("pgp-signature");
1540  mutt_param_set(&t->parameter, "name", "signature.asc");
1541  t->use_disp = false;
1542  t->disposition = DISP_NONE;
1543  t->encoding = ENC_7BIT;
1544  }
1545  t->filename = sigfile;
1546  t->unlink = true; /* ok to remove this file after sending. */
1547 
1548  return a;
1549 }

static int show_sig_summary ( unsigned long  sum,

gpgme_key_t  key,
int  idx,

)
static

Show a signature summary.

Parameters
sumFlags, e.g. GPGME_SIGSUM_KEY_REVOKED
ctxGPGME handle
keySet of keys
idxIndex into key set
sState to use
sigGPGME signature
Return values
0Success
1There is a severe warning

1666  bool severe = false;

1673

1698  ;
1699 
1700  state_puts(s, _("Warning: The signature expired at: "));
1701  print_time(sig2 ? sig2->exp_timestamp : 0, s);
1702  state_puts(s, "\n");

1704 
1705  if ((sum & GPGME_SIGSUM_KEY_MISSING))
1706  {
1707  state_puts(s, _("Can't verify due to a missing key or certificate\n"));

1709 
1710  if ((sum & GPGME_SIGSUM_CRL_MISSING))

1715

1717  {

1721

1723  state_puts(s, _("A policy requirement was not met\n"));

1732  state_puts(s, _("A system error occurred"));

1737  ;

1740  t0 = "";
1741  t1 = sig2->wrong_key_usage ? "Wrong_Key_Usage" : "";

1743 
1744  if (t0 || t1)
1745  {
1746  state_puts(s, ": ");
1747  if (t0)
1748  state_puts(s, t0);
1749  if (t1 && !(t0 && (strcmp(t0, t1) == 0)))
1750  {
1751  if (t0)
1752  state_puts(s, ",");
1753  state_puts(s, t1);
1754  }
1755  }
1756  state_puts(s, "\n");

1758 
1759  if (C_CryptUsePka)

1762  {

1765  state_puts(s, "\n");

1771  state_puts(s, "\n");
1772  }

1775  return severe;

1784 {
1785  if (!key)
1786  return;
1787 
1788  const char *prefix = _("Fingerprint: ");
1789 
1790  const char *s = key->subkeys ? key->subkeys->fpr : NULL;
1791  if (!s)
1792  return;
1793  bool is_pgp = (key->protocol == GPGME_PROTOCOL_OpenPGP);
1794 
1795  char *buf = mutt_mem_malloc(strlen(prefix) + strlen(s) * 4 + 2);
1796  strcpy(buf, prefix);
1797  char *p = buf + strlen(buf);
1798  if (is_pgp && (strlen(s) == 40))
1799  { /* PGP v4 style formatted. */
1800  for (int i = 0; *s && s[1] && s[2] && s[3] && s[4]; s += 4, i++)
1801  {
1802  *p++ = s[0];
1803  *p++ = s[1];
1804  *p++ = s[2];
1805  *p++ = s[3];
1806  *p++ = ' ';
1807  if (i == 4)
1808  *p++ = ' ';
1809  }
1810  }
1811  else
1812  {
1813  for (int i = 0; *s && s[1] && s[2]; s += 2, i++)
1814  {
1815  *p++ = s[0];
1816  *p++ = s[1];
1817  *p++ = is_pgp ? ' ' : ':';
1818  if (is_pgp && (i == 7))
1819  *p++ = ' ';
1820  }
1821  }
1822 
1823  /* just in case print remaining odd digits */
1824  for (; *s; s++)
1825  *p++ = *s;
1826  *p++ = '\n';
1827  *p = '\0';
1828  state_puts(state, buf);
1829  FREE(&buf);
1830 }

1855  case GPGME_VALIDITY_UNDEFINED:
1856  break;
1857  case GPGME_VALIDITY_NEVER:
1858  txt = _("WARNING: The key does NOT BELONG to "
1859  "the person named as shown above\n");
1860  break;
1861  case GPGME_VALIDITY_MARGINAL:
1862  txt = _("WARNING: It is NOT certain that the key "

1866  case GPGME_VALIDITY_ULTIMATE:

1890  {
1891  bool aka = false;

1893  {

1895  continue;

1897  {
1898  msgwid = mutt_strwidth(msg) - mutt_strwidth(_("aka: ")) + 1;

1900  msgwid = 0;

1902  state_puts(s, " ");

1904  }
1905  state_puts(s, uids->uid);
1906  state_puts(s, "\n");
1907

1909  }
1910  }
1911  else
1912  {
1913  if (sig->fpr)
1914  {
1915  state_puts(s, _("KeyID "));
1916  state_puts(s, sig->fpr);
1917  }
1918  else
1919  {
1920  /* L10N: You will see this message in place of "KeyID "
1921  if the S/MIME key has no ID. This is quite an error. */
1922  state_puts(s, _("no signature fingerprint available"));
1923  }
1924  state_puts(s, "\n");
1925  }
1926 
1927  /* timestamp is 0 when verification failed.
1928  * "Jan 1 1970" is not the created date. */
1929  if (sig->timestamp)
1930  {
1931  msgwid = mutt_strwidth(msg) - mutt_strwidth(_("created: ")) + 1;
1932  if (msgwid < 0)
1933  msgwid = 0;
1934  for (int i = 0; i < msgwid; i++)
1935  state_puts(s, " ");
1936  state_puts(s, _("created: "));
1937  print_time(sig->timestamp, s);
1938  state_puts(s, "\n");

◆ show_one_sig_status()

static int show_one_sig_status ( gpgme_ctx_t  ctx,
int  idx,
struct State *  s 
)
static

Show information about one signature.

Parameters
ctxGPGME handle of a successful verification
idxIndex
sState to use
Return values
0Normal procession
1A bad signature
2A signature with a warning
-1No more signature

The index should start at 0 and increment for each call/signature.

1955 {
1956  const char *fpr = NULL;
1957  gpgme_key_t key = NULL;
1958  bool anybad = false, anywarn = false;
1959  gpgme_signature_t sig = NULL;
1960  gpgme_error_t err = GPG_ERR_NO_ERROR;
1961 
1962  gpgme_verify_result_t result = gpgme_op_verify_result(ctx);
1963  if (result)
1964  {
1965  /* FIXME: this code should use a static variable and remember
1966  * the current position in the list of signatures, IMHO.
1967  * -moritz. */
1968  int i;
1969  for (i = 0, sig = result->signatures; sig && (i < idx); i++, sig = sig->next)
1970  ;
1971  if (!sig)
1972  return -1; /* Signature not found. */
1973 
1974  if (signature_key)
1975  {
1976  gpgme_key_unref(signature_key);
1977  signature_key = NULL;
1978  }
1979 
1980  fpr = sig->fpr;
1981  const unsigned int sum = sig->summary;
1982 
1983  if (gpg_err_code(sig->status) != GPG_ERR_NO_ERROR)
1984  anybad = true;
1985 
1986  if (gpg_err_code(sig->status) != GPG_ERR_NO_PUBKEY)
1987  {
1988  err = gpgme_get_key(ctx, fpr, &key, 0); /* secret key? */
1989  if (err == 0)
1990  {
1991  if (!signature_key)
1992  signature_key = key;
1993  }
1994  else
1995  {
1996  key = NULL; /* Old GPGME versions did not set KEY to NULL on
1997  error. Do it here to avoid a double free. */
1998  }
1999  }
2000  else
2001  {
2002  /* pubkey not present */
2003  }
2004 
2005  if (!s || !s->fp_out || !(s->flags & MUTT_DISPLAY))
2006  ; /* No state information so no way to print anything. */
2007  else if (err != 0)
2008  {
2009  char buf[1024];
2010  snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), _("Error getting key information for KeyID %s: %s\n"),
2011  fpr, gpgme_strerror(err));
2012  state_puts(s, buf);
2013  anybad = true;
2014  }
2015  else if ((sum & GPGME_SIGSUM_GREEN))
2016  {
2017  print_smime_keyinfo(_("Good signature from:"), sig, key, s);
2018  if (show_sig_summary(sum, ctx, key, idx, s, sig))
2019  anywarn = true;
2020  show_one_sig_validity(ctx, idx, s);
2021  }
2022  else if ((sum & GPGME_SIGSUM_RED))
2023  {
2024  print_smime_keyinfo(_("*BAD* signature from:"), sig, key, s);
2025  show_sig_summary(sum, ctx, key, idx, s, sig);
2026  }
2027  else if (!anybad && key && (key->protocol == GPGME_PROTOCOL_OpenPGP))
2028  { /* We can't decide (yellow) but this is a PGP key with a good
2029  signature, so we display what a PGP user expects: The name,
2030  fingerprint and the key validity (which is neither fully or
2031  ultimate). */
2032  print_smime_keyinfo(_("Good signature from:"), sig, key, s);
2033  show_one_sig_validity(ctx, idx, s);
2034  show_fingerprint(key, s);
2035  if (show_sig_summary(sum, ctx, key, idx, s, sig))
2036  anywarn = true;
2037  }
2038  else /* can't decide (yellow) */
2039  {
2040  print_smime_keyinfo(_("Problem signature from:"), sig, key, s);
2041  /* 0 indicates no expiration */
2042  if (sig->exp_timestamp)
2043  {
2044  /* L10N: This is trying to match the width of the
2045  "Problem signature from:" translation just above. */
2046  state_puts(s, _(" expires: "));
2047  print_time(sig->exp_timestamp, s);
2048  state_puts(s, "\n");
2049  }
2050  show_sig_summary(sum, ctx, key, idx, s, sig);
2051  anywarn = true;
2052  }
2053 
2054  if (key != signature_key)
2055  gpgme_key_unref(key);
2056  }
2057 
2058  return anybad ? 1 : anywarn ? 2 : 0;
2059 }

2166  {
2167  if (is_pka_notation(notation))

◆ decrypt_part()

static struct Body* decrypt_part ( struct Body *  a,
struct State *  s,
FILE *  fp_out,
bool  is_smime,
int *  r_is_signed 
)
static

Decrypt a PGP or SMIME message.

Parameters
[in]aBody of message
[in]sState to use
[in]fp_outFile to write to
[in]is_smimeTrue if an SMIME message
[out]r_is_signedFlag, R_IS_SIGNED (PGP only)
Return values
ptrNewly allocated Body

For PGP returns a flag in R_IS_SIGNED to indicate whether this is a combined encrypted and signed message, for S/MIME it returns true when it is not a encrypted but a signed message.

2483  return -1;

2485  if (b->parts)
2486  return -1;

2489  * backend. The backend allows for Base64 encoded data but it does
2490  * not allow for QP which I have seen in some messages. So better
2491  * do it here. */
2492  saved_b_type = b->type;
2493  saved_b_offset = b->offset;
2494  saved_b_length = b->length;

2502  }
2503

2505  mutt_decode_attachment(b, &s);
2506  fflush(fp_tmp);
2507  b->length = ftello(s.fp_out);
2508  b->offset = 0;
2509  rewind(fp_tmp);
2510 
2511  memset(&s, 0, sizeof(s));
2512  s.fp_in = fp_tmp;
2513  s.fp_out = 0;
2514  *fp_out = mutt_file_mkstemp();
2515  if (!*fp_out)
2516  {
2517  mutt_perror(_("Can't create temporary file"));
2518  return -1;
2519  }
2520 
2521  *cur = decrypt_part(b, &s, *fp_out, true, &is_signed);
2522  if (*cur)
2523  (*cur)->goodsig = is_signed > 0;
2524  b->type = saved_b_type;
2525  b->length = saved_b_length;
2526  b->offset = saved_b_offset;
2527  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp);
2528  rewind(*fp_out);
2529  if (*cur && !is_signed && !(*cur)->parts && mutt_is_application_smime(*cur))
2530  {
2531  /* Assume that this is a opaque signed s/mime message. This is an ugly way
2532  * of doing it but we have anyway a problem with arbitrary encoded S/MIME
2533  * messages: Only the outer part may be encrypted. The entire mime parsing
2534  * should be revamped, probably by keeping the temporary files so that we
2535  * don't need to decrypt them all the time. Inner parts of an encrypted
2536  * part can then point into this file and there won't ever be a need to
2537  * decrypt again. This needs a partial rewrite of the MIME engine. */
2538  struct Body *bb = *cur;
2539 
2540  saved_b_type = bb->type;
2541  saved_b_offset = bb->offset;
2542  saved_b_length = bb->length;
2543  memset(&s, 0, sizeof(s));
2544  s.fp_in = *fp_out;
2545  fseeko(s.fp_in, bb->offset, SEEK_SET);

2547  if (!fp_tmp2)
2548  {
2549  mutt_perror(_("Can't create temporary file"));
2550  return -1;
2551  }
2552 
2553  s.fp_out = fp_tmp2;
2554  mutt_decode_attachment(bb, &s);
2555  fflush(fp_tmp2);
2556  bb->length = ftello(s.fp_out);
2557  bb->offset = 0;
2558  rewind(fp_tmp2);
2559  mutt_file_fclose(fp_out);
2560 
2561  memset(&s, 0, sizeof(s));
2562  s.fp_in = fp_tmp2;
2563  s.fp_out = 0;
2564  *fp_out = mutt_file_mkstemp();
2565  if (!*fp_out)
2566  {
2567  mutt_perror(_("Can't create temporary file"));
2568  return -1;
2569  }
2570 
2571  struct Body *b_tmp = decrypt_part(bb, &s, *fp_out, true, &is_signed);
2572  if (b_tmp)
2573  b_tmp->goodsig = is_signed > 0;
2574  bb->type = saved_b_type;
2575  bb->length = saved_b_length;
2576  bb->offset = saved_b_offset;
2577  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_tmp2);
2578  rewind(*fp_out);
2579  mutt_body_free(cur);
2580  *cur = b_tmp;
2581  }
2582  return *cur ? 0 : -1;

2594  /* Before GPGME 1.9.0 and gpg 2.1.14 there was no side-effect free
2595  * way to view key data in GPGME, so we import the key into a
2596  * temporary keyring if we detect an older system. */
2597  bool legacy_api;
2598  struct Buffer *tmpdir = NULL;
2599  gpgme_ctx_t tmpctx = NULL;
2600  gpgme_error_t err;
2601  gpgme_engine_info_t engineinfo = NULL;

2603  gpgme_user_id_t uid = NULL;
2604  gpgme_subkey_t subkey = NULL;

2609  int rc = -1;
2610  time_t tt;
2611 
2612 #if GPGME_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x010900 /* GPGME >= 1.9.0 */
2613  legacy_api = !have_gpg_version("2.1.14");
2614 #else /* GPGME < 1.9.0 */
2615  legacy_api = true;
2616 #endif
2617 
2618  tmpctx = create_gpgme_context(false);
2619 
2620  if (legacy_api)
2621  {
2622  tmpdir = mutt_buffer_pool_get();
2623  mutt_buffer_printf(tmpdir, "%s/neomutt-gpgme-XXXXXX", NONULL(C_Tmpdir));
2624  if (!mkdtemp(tmpdir->data))
2625  {
2626  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Error creating temporary GPGME home\n");
2627  goto err_ctx;
2628  }
2629 
2630  engineinfo = gpgme_ctx_get_engine_info(tmpctx);
2631  while (engineinfo && (engineinfo->protocol != GPGME_PROTOCOL_OpenPGP))
2632  engineinfo = engineinfo->next;
2633  if (!engineinfo)
2634  {
2635  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Error finding GPGME PGP engine\n");
2636  goto err_tmpdir;
2637  }
2638 
2639  err = gpgme_ctx_set_engine_info(tmpctx, GPGME_PROTOCOL_OpenPGP,
2640  engineinfo->file_name, mutt_b2s(tmpdir));
2641  if (err != GPG_ERR_NO_ERROR)
2642  {
2643  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Error setting GPGME context home\n");
2644  goto err_tmpdir;
2645  }
2646  }
2647 
2648  *fp = mutt_file_mkstemp();
2649  if (!*fp)
2650  {
2651  mutt_perror(_("Can't create temporary file"));
2652  goto err_tmpdir;
2653  }
2654 
2655 #if GPGME_VERSION_NUMBER >= 0x010900 /* 1.9.0 */
2656  if (!legacy_api)
2657  err = gpgme_op_keylist_from_data_start(tmpctx, keydata, 0);
2658  else
2659 #endif /* GPGME >= 1.9.0 */
2660  {
2661  err = gpgme_op_keylist_start(tmpctx, NULL, 0);
2662  }
2663  while (err == 0)
2664  {
2665  err = gpgme_op_keylist_next(tmpctx, &key);
2666  if (err != 0)
2667  break;
2668  uid = key->uids;
2669  subkey = key->subkeys;
2670  more = false;
2671  while (subkey)
2672  {
2673  shortid = subkey->keyid;
2674  len = mutt_str_strlen(subkey->keyid);
2675  if (len > 8)
2676  shortid += len - 8;
2677  tt = subkey->timestamp;
2678  mutt_date_localtime_format(date, sizeof(date), "%Y-%m-%d", tt);
2679 
2680  if (more)
2681  {
2682  fprintf(*fp, "sub %5.5s %u/%8s %s\n", gpgme_pubkey_algo_name(subkey->pubkey_algo),
2683  subkey->length, shortid, date);
2684  }
2685  else
2686  {
2687  fprintf(*fp, "pub %5.5s %u/%8s %s %s\n", gpgme_pubkey_algo_name(subkey->pubkey_algo),
2688  subkey->length, shortid, date, uid->uid);
2689  }
2690  subkey = subkey->next;
2691  more = true;
2692  }
2693  gpgme_key_unref(key);
2694  }
2695  if (gpg_err_code(err) != GPG_ERR_EOF)
2696  {
2697  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG1, "Error listing keys\n");
2698  goto err_fp;
2699  }
2700 
2701  rc = 0;
2702 
2703 err_fp:
2704  if (rc)
2705  mutt_file_fclose(fp);
2706 err_tmpdir:
2707  if (legacy_api)
2708  mutt_file_rmtree(mutt_b2s(tmpdir));
2709 err_ctx:
2710  gpgme_release(tmpctx);

2714  return rc;

2836 {
2837  gpgme_ctx_t ctx = create_gpgme_context(false);

2840  gpgme_import_status_t st;
2841  bool any;
2842 
2843  FILE *fp_in = mutt_file_fopen(fname, "r");
2844  if (!fp_in)
2845  {
2846  mutt_perror(fname);
2847  goto leave;
2848  }
2849  /* Note that the stream, "fp_in", needs to be kept open while the keydata
2850  * is used. */
2851  gpgme_error_t err = gpgme_data_new_from_stream(&keydata, fp_in);
2852  if (err != GPG_ERR_NO_ERROR)
2853  {
2854  mutt_error(_("error allocating data object: %s"), gpgme_strerror(err));
2855  goto leave;
2856  }
2857 
2858  err = gpgme_op_import(ctx, keydata);
2859  if (err != 0)
2860  {
2861  mutt_error(_("Error importing key: %s"), gpgme_strerror(err));
2862  goto leave;
2863  }
2864 
2865  /* Print infos about the imported keys to stdout. */
2866  impres = gpgme_op_import_result(ctx);
2867  if (!impres)
2868  {
2869  fputs("oops: no import result returned\n", stdout);
2870  goto leave;
2871  }
2872 
2873  for (st = impres->imports; st; st = st->next)
2874  {
2875  if (st->result)
2876  continue;
2877  printf("key %s imported (", NONULL(st->fpr));
2878  /* Note that we use the singular even if it is possible that
2879  * several uids etc are new. This simply looks better. */
2880  any = false;
2881  if (st->status & GPGME_IMPORT_SECRET)
2882  {
2883  printf("secret parts");
2884  any = true;
2885  }
2886  if ((st->status & GPGME_IMPORT_NEW))
2887  {
2888  printf("%snew key", any ? ", " : "");
2889  any = true;
2890  }
2891  if ((st->status & GPGME_IMPORT_UID))
2892  {
2893  printf("%snew uid", any ? ", " : "");
2894  any = true;
2895  }
2896  if ((st->status & GPGME_IMPORT_SIG))
2897  {
2898  printf("%snew sig", any ? ", " : "");
2899  any = true;
2900  }
2901  if ((st->status & GPGME_IMPORT_SUBKEY))
2902  {
2903  printf("%snew subkey", any ? ", " : "");
2904  any = true;
2905  }
2906  printf("%s)\n", any ? "" : "not changed");
2907  /* Fixme: Should we lookup each imported key and print more infos? */
2908  }
2909  /* Now print keys which failed the import. Unfortunately in most
2910  * cases gpg will bail out early and not tell GPGME about. */
2911  /* FIXME: We could instead use the new GPGME_AUDITLOG_DIAG to show
2912  * the actual gpg diagnostics. But I fear that would clutter the
2913  * output too much. Maybe a dedicated prompt or option to do this
2914  * would be helpful. */
2915  for (st = impres->imports; st; st = st->next)
2916  {
2917  if (st->result == 0)
2918  continue;
2919  printf("key %s import failed: %s\n", NONULL(st->fpr), gpgme_strerror(st->result));
2920  }
2921  fflush(stdout);
2922 
2923 leave:
2924  gpgme_release(ctx);
2925  gpgme_data_release(keydata);
2926  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_in);
2927 }

◆ copy_clearsigned()

static void copy_clearsigned ( gpgme_data_t  data,
struct State *  s,
char *  charset 
)
static

Copy a clearsigned message.

Parameters
dataGPGME data object
sState to use
charsetCharset of message

strip the signature and PGP's dash-escaping.

XXX charset handling: We assume that it is safe to do character set decoding first, dash decoding second here, while we do it the other way around in the main handler.

(Note that we aren't worse than Outlook & Cie in this, and also note that we can successfully handle anything produced by any existing versions of neomutt.)

3007  char buf[8192];
3008  FILE *fp_out = NULL;
3009 
3010  gpgme_error_t err = 0;
3011  gpgme_data_t armored_data = NULL;
3012 
3013  bool maybe_goodsig = true;
3014  bool have_any_sigs = false;
3015 
3016  char body_charset[256]; /* Only used for clearsigned messages. */
3017 
3018  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG2, "Entering handler\n");
3019 
3020  /* For clearsigned messages we won't be able to get a character set
3021  * but we know that this may only be text thus we assume Latin-1 here. */
3022  if (!mutt_body_get_charset(m, body_charset, sizeof(body_charset)))
3023  mutt_str_strfcpy(body_charset, "iso-8859-1", sizeof(body_charset));
3024 
3025  fseeko(s->fp_in, m->offset, SEEK_SET);
3026  last_pos = m->offset;
3027 
3028  for (bytes = m->length; bytes > 0;)
3029  {
3030  if (!fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), s->fp_in))
3031  break;
3032 
3033  LOFF_T offset = ftello(s->fp_in);
3034  bytes -= (offset - last_pos); /* don't rely on mutt_str_strlen(buf) */
3035  last_pos = offset;
3036 
3037  size_t plen = mutt_str_startswith(buf, "-----BEGIN PGP ", CASE_MATCH);
3038  if (plen != 0)
3039  {
3040  clearsign = false;
3041 
3042  if (MESSAGE(buf + plen))
3043  needpass = 1;
3044  else if (SIGNED_MESSAGE(buf + plen))
3045  {
3046  clearsign = true;
3047  needpass = 0;
3048  }
3049  else if (PUBLIC_KEY_BLOCK(buf + plen))
3050  {
3051  needpass = 0;
3052  pgp_keyblock = true;
3053  }
3054  else
3055  {
3056  /* XXX we may wish to recode here */
3057  if (s->prefix)
3058  state_puts(s, s->prefix);
3059  state_puts(s, buf);
3060  continue;
3061  }
3062 
3063  have_any_sigs = (have_any_sigs || (clearsign && (s->flags & MUTT_VERIFY)));
3064 
3065  /* Copy PGP material to an data container */
3066  armored_data = file_to_data_object(s->fp_in, m->offset, m->length);
3067  /* Invoke PGP if needed */
3068  if (pgp_keyblock)
3069  {
3070  pgp_gpgme_extract_keys(armored_data, &fp_out);
3071  }
3072  else if (!clearsign || (s->flags & MUTT_VERIFY))
3073  {
3074  gpgme_data_t plaintext = create_gpgme_data();
3075  gpgme_ctx_t ctx = create_gpgme_context(false);
3076 
3077  if (clearsign)
3078  err = gpgme_op_verify(ctx, armored_data, NULL, plaintext);
3079  else
3080  {
3081  err = gpgme_op_decrypt_verify(ctx, armored_data, plaintext);
3082  if (gpg_err_code(err) == GPG_ERR_NO_DATA)
3083  {
3084  /* Decrypt verify can't handle signed only messages. */
3085  gpgme_data_seek(armored_data, 0, SEEK_SET);
3086  /* Must release plaintext so that we supply an uninitialized object. */
3087  gpgme_data_release(plaintext);
3088  plaintext = create_gpgme_data();
3089  err = gpgme_op_verify(ctx, armored_data, NULL, plaintext);
3090  }
3091  }
3092  redraw_if_needed(ctx);
3093 
3094  if (err != 0)
3095  {
3096  char errbuf[200];
3097 
3098  snprintf(errbuf, sizeof(errbuf) - 1,
3099  _("Error: decryption/verification failed: %s\n"), gpgme_strerror(err));
3100  state_puts(s, errbuf);
3101  }
3102  else
3103  {
3104  /* Decryption/Verification succeeded */
3105 
3106  mutt_message(_("PGP message successfully decrypted"));
3107 
3108  bool sig_stat = false;
3109  char *tmpfname = NULL;
3110 
3111  {
3112  /* Check whether signatures have been verified. */
3113  gpgme_verify_result_t verify_result = gpgme_op_verify_result(ctx);
3114  if (verify_result->signatures)
3115  sig_stat = true;
3116  }
3117 
3118  have_any_sigs = false;
3119  maybe_goodsig = false;
3120  if ((s->flags & MUTT_DISPLAY) && sig_stat)
3121  {
3122  int res, idx;
3123  bool anybad = false;
3124 
3125  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- Begin signature information --]\n"));
3126  have_any_sigs = true;
3127  for (idx = 0; (res = show_one_sig_status(ctx, idx, s)) != -1; idx++)
3128  {
3129  if (res == 1)
3130  anybad = true;
3131  }
3132  if (!anybad && idx)
3133  maybe_goodsig = true;
3134 
3135  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- End signature information --]\n\n"));
3136  }
3137 
3138  tmpfname = data_object_to_tempfile(plaintext, &fp_out);
3139  if (tmpfname)
3140  {
3141  unlink(tmpfname);
3142  FREE(&tmpfname);
3143  }
3144  else
3145  {
3146  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_out);
3147  state_puts(s, _("Error: copy data failed\n"));
3148  }
3149  }
3150  gpgme_data_release(plaintext);
3151  gpgme_release(ctx);
3152  }
3153 
3154  /* Now, copy cleartext to the screen. NOTE - we expect that PGP
3155  * outputs utf-8 cleartext. This may not always be true, but it
3156  * seems to be a reasonable guess. */
3157  if (s->flags & MUTT_DISPLAY)
3158  {
3159  if (needpass)
3160  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- BEGIN PGP MESSAGE --]\n\n"));
3161  else if (pgp_keyblock)
3162  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK --]\n"));
3163  else
3164  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE --]\n\n"));
3165  }
3166 
3167  if (clearsign)
3168  {
3169  copy_clearsigned(armored_data, s, body_charset);
3170  }
3171  else if (fp_out)
3172  {
3173  int c;
3174  rewind(fp_out);
3175  struct FgetConv *fc = mutt_ch_fgetconv_open(fp_out, "utf-8", C_Charset, 0);
3176  while ((c = mutt_ch_fgetconv(fc)) != EOF)
3177  {
3178  state_putc(s, c);
3179  if ((c == '\n') && s->prefix)
3180  state_puts(s, s->prefix);
3181  }
3182  mutt_ch_fgetconv_close(&fc);
3183  }
3184 
3185  if (s->flags & MUTT_DISPLAY)
3186  {
3187  state_putc(s, '\n');
3188  if (needpass)
3189  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- END PGP MESSAGE --]\n"));
3190  else if (pgp_keyblock)
3191  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK --]\n"));
3192  else
3193  state_attach_puts(s, _("[-- END PGP SIGNED MESSAGE --]\n"));
3194  }
3195 
3196  gpgme_data_release(armored_data);
3197  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_out);
3198  }
3199  else
3200  {
3201  /* A traditional PGP part may mix signed and unsigned content */
3202  /* XXX we may wish to recode here */
3203  if (s->prefix)
3204  state_puts(s, s->prefix);
3205  state_puts(s, buf);
3206  }
3207  }
3208 
3209  m->goodsig = (maybe_goodsig && have_any_sigs);
3210 
3211  if (needpass == -1)
3212  {
3213  state_attach_puts(
3214  s, _("[-- Error: could not find beginning of PGP message --]\n\n"));
3215  return 1;
3216  }
3217  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG2, "Leaving handler\n");
3218 
3219  return err;
3220 }

3237  {

3240  {
3241  state_attach_puts(s,

3246

3248  if (tattach)
3249  {
3250  tattach->goodsig = is_signed > 0;
3251 
3252  if (s->flags & MUTT_DISPLAY)

3254  state_attach_puts(
3255  s, is_signed ?
3256  _("[-- The following data is PGP/MIME signed and encrypted "
3257  "--]\n\n") :
3258  _("[-- The following data is PGP/MIME encrypted --]\n\n"));
3259  mutt_protected_headers_handler(tattach, s);
3260  }
3261 
3262  /* Store any protected headers in the parent so they can be
3263  * accessed for index updates after the handler recursion is done.
3264  * This is done before the handler to prevent a nested encrypted
3265  * handler from freeing the headers. */
3266  mutt_env_free(&a->mime_headers);
3267  a->mime_headers = tattach->mime_headers;
3268  tattach->mime_headers = NULL;
3269 
3270  {
3271  FILE *fp_save = s->fp_in;
3272  s->fp_in = fp_out;
3273  rc = mutt_body_handler(tattach, s);
3274  s->fp_in = fp_save;
3275  }
3276 
3277  /* Embedded multipart signed protected headers override the
3278  * encrypted headers. We need to do this after the handler so
3279  * they can be printed in the pager. */
3280  if (mutt_is_multipart_signed(tattach) && tattach->parts && tattach->parts->mime_headers)
3281  {
3282  mutt_env_free(&a->mime_headers);
3283  a->mime_headers = tattach->parts->mime_headers;
3284  tattach->parts->mime_headers = NULL;
3285  }
3286 
3287  /* if a multipart/signed is the _only_ sub-part of a
3288  * multipart/encrypted, cache signature verification
3289  * status. */
3290  if (mutt_is_multipart_signed(tattach) && !tattach->next)
3291  a->goodsig |= tattach->goodsig;
3292 
3293  if (s->flags & MUTT_DISPLAY)
3294  {
3295  state_puts(s, "\n");
3296  state_attach_puts(
3297  s, is_signed ?
3298  _("[-- End of PGP/MIME signed and encrypted data --]\n") :
3299  _("[-- End of PGP/MIME encrypted data --]\n"));
3300  }
3301 
3302  mutt_body_free(&tattach);
3303  mutt_message(_("PGP message successfully decrypted"));
3304  }
3305  else
3306  {
3307 #ifdef USE_AUTOCRYPT
3308  if (!OptAutocryptGpgme)
3309 #endif
3310  {
3311  mutt_error(_("Could not decrypt PGP message"));
3312  }
3313  rc = -1;
3314  }
3315 
3316  mutt_file_fclose(&fp_out);
3317  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG2, "Leaving handler\n");

Format a string for the key selection menu - Implements format_t.

Expando Description
%n Number
%p Protocol
%t Trust/validity of the key-uid association
%u User id
%[fmt] Date of key using strftime(3)

| | | %a | Algorithm | %c | Capabilities | %f | Flags | %k | Key id | %l | Length | | | %A | Algorithm of the principal key | %C | Capabilities of the principal key | %F | Flags of the principal key | %K | Key id of the principal key | %L | Length of the principal key

3481

3487  }

3489  optional = false;

3501 
3502  case 'l':
3503  if (!optional)
3504  {

3508  val = key->kobj->subkeys->length;
3509  else
3510  val = 0;

3513  break;
3514 
3515  case 'n':
3516  if (!optional)
3517  {
3518  snprintf(fmt, sizeof(fmt), "%%%sd", prec);

3522 
3523  case 'p':

3527 
3528  case 't':
3529  {
3530  char *s = NULL;

3536  {
3537  case GPGME_VALIDITY_FULL:
3538  s = "f";

3540  case GPGME_VALIDITY_MARGINAL:
3541  s = "m";
3542  break;
3543  case GPGME_VALIDITY_NEVER:
3544  s = "n";
3545  break;
3546  case GPGME_VALIDITY_ULTIMATE:
3547  s = "u";
3548  break;
3549  case GPGME_VALIDITY_UNDEFINED:
3550  s = "q";
3551  break;
3552  case GPGME_VALIDITY_UNKNOWN:
3553  default:
3554  s = "?";
3555  break;
3556  }
3557  }

3561  }
3562 
3563  case 'u':

3571  case '[':
3572  {

3577  char *p = buf;

3579  const char *cp = src;
3580  if (*cp == '!')
3581  {
3582  do_locales = false;
3583  cp++;
3584  }
3585 
3586  size_t len = buflen - 1;
3587  while ((len > 0) && (*cp != ']'))
3588  {
3589  if (*cp == '%')
3590  {
3591  cp++;
3592  if (len >= 2)
3593  {
3594  *p++ = '%';
3595  *p++ = *cp;
3596  len -= 2;
3597  }
3598  else
3599  break; /* not enough space */
3600  cp++;
3601  }
3602  else
3603  {
3604  *p++ = *cp++;
3605  len--;
3606  }
3607  }
3608  *p = '\0';
3609 
3610  if (key->kobj->subkeys && (key->kobj->subkeys->timestamp > 0))
3611  tm = mutt_date_localtime(key->kobj->subkeys->timestamp);
3612  else
3613  tm = mutt_date_localtime(0); // Default to 1970-01-01
3614 
3615  if (!do_locales)
3616  setlocale(LC_TIME, "C");
3617  strftime(buf2, sizeof(buf2), buf, &tm);
3618  if (!do_locales)
3619  setlocale(LC_TIME, "");
3620 
3621  snprintf(fmt, sizeof(fmt), "%%%ss", prec);
3622  snprintf(buf, buflen, fmt, buf2);
3623  if (len > 0)
3624  src = cp + 1;
3625  break;
3626  }
3627 
3628  default:
3629  *buf = '\0';
3630  }
3631 
3632  if (optional)
3633  {
3634  mutt_expando_format(buf, buflen, col, cols, if_str, attach_format_str, data,
3635  MUTT_FORMAT_NO_FLAGS);
3636  }

3638  {
3639  mutt_expando_format(buf, buflen, col, cols, else_str, attach_format_str,
3640  data, MUTT_FORMAT_NO_FLAGS);
3641  }
3642  return src;
3643 }

◆ parse_dn()

static struct DnArray* parse_dn ( const char *  str)
static

Parse a DN and return an array-ized one.

Parameters
strString to parse
Return values
ptrArray of Distinguished Names

This is not a validating parser and it does not support any old-stylish syntax; GPGME is expected to return only rfc2253 compatible strings.

4016  {
4017  while (str[0] == ' ')
4018  str++;

4020  break; /* ready */

4022  {

◆ parse_and_print_user_id()

static void parse_and_print_user_id ( FILE *  fp,
const char *  userid 
)
static

Print a nice representation of the userid.

Parameters
fpFile to write to
useridString returned by GPGME key functions (utf-8 encoded)

Make sure it is displayed in a proper way, which does mean to reorder some parts for S/MIME's DNs.

4080  fputs(_("[Can't display this user ID (unknown encoding)]"), fp);

4417  int maxdepth = 100;
4418 
4419  struct Buffer tempfile = mutt_buffer_make(PATH_MAX);
4420  mutt_buffer_mktemp(&tempfile);
4421  FILE *fp = mutt_file_fopen(mutt_b2s(&tempfile), "w");
4422  if (!fp)
4423  {
4424  mutt_perror(_("Can't create temporary file"));

4426  }
4427  mutt_message(_("Collecting data..."));

4431  listctx = create_gpgme_context(key->flags & KEYFLAG_ISX509);
4432 
4433  k = key->kobj;
4434  gpgme_key_ref(k);
4435  while ((s = k->chain_id) && k->subkeys && (strcmp(s, k->subkeys->fpr) != 0))
4436  {
4437  putc('\n', fp);
4438  err = gpgme_op_keylist_start(listctx, s, 0);
4439  gpgme_key_unref(k);
4440  k = NULL;
4441  if (err == 0)
4442  err = gpgme_op_keylist_next(listctx, &k);
4443  if (err != 0)
4444  {
4445  fprintf(fp, _("Error finding issuer key: %s\n"), gpgme_strerror(err));
4446  goto leave;
4447  }
4448  gpgme_op_keylist_end(listctx);
4449 
4450  print_key_info(k, fp);
4451  if (!--maxdepth)
4452  {
4453  putc('\n', fp);
4454  fputs(_("Error: certification chain too long - stopping here\n"), fp);
4455  break;
4456  }
4457  }
4458 
4459 leave:
4460  gpgme_key_unref(k);
4461  gpgme_release(listctx);
4462  mutt_file_fclose(&fp);
4463  mutt_clear_error();
4464  snprintf(cmd, sizeof(cmd), _("Key ID: 0x%s"), crypt_keyid(key));
4465  mutt_do_pager(cmd, mutt_b2s(&tempfile), MUTT_PAGER_NO_FLAGS, NULL);
4466 
4467 cleanup:
4468  mutt_buffer_dealloc(&tempfile);
4469 }

static char* list_to_pattern ( struct ListHead *  list)
static

Convert STailQ to GPGME-compatible pattern.

Parameters
listList of strings to convert
Return values
ptrGPGME-compatible pattern

We need to convert spaces in an item into a '+' and '' into "%25".

Note
The caller must free the returned pattern

4482  char *pattern = NULL, *p = NULL;

4486  n = 0;
4487  struct ListNode *np = NULL;
4488  STAILQ_FOREACH(np, list, entries)
4489  {

4491  {
4492  if ((*s == '%') || (*s == '+'))
4493  n += 2;
4494  n++;
4495  }
4496  n++; /* delimiter or end of string */
4497  }
4498  n++; /* make sure to allocate at least one byte */

4500  pattern = p;
4501  STAILQ_FOREACH(np, list, entries)
4502  {
4503  s = np->data;
4504  if (*s)
4505  {
4506  if (np != STAILQ_FIRST(list))

4509  {
4510  if (*s == '%')
4511  {
4512  *p++ = '%';
4513  *p++ = '2';

4527  }
4528  }
4529  *p = '\0';

Return values
ptrKey List
NULLError

Select by looking at the HINTS list.

◆ crypt_add_string_to_hints()

static void crypt_add_string_to_hints ( const char *  str,
struct ListHead *  hints 
)
static

Split a string and add the parts to a List.

Parameters
[in]strString to parse
[out]hintsList of string parts

The string str is split by whitespace and punctuation and the parts added to hints. This list is later used to match addresses.

static

Get the user to select a key.

Parameters
[in]keysList of keys to select from
[in]pAddress to match
[in]sReal name to display
[in]appFlags, e.g. APPLICATION_PGP

Return values
ptrKey selected by user

Display a menu to select a key from the array of keys.

4815  dialog_push(dlg);
4816 
4817  struct Menu *menu = mutt_menu_new(menu_to_use);

4819  menu->pagelen = index->state.rows;

4822 
4823  menu->max = i;

4826  menu->data = key_table;

4829  {
4830  const char *ts = NULL;

4832  if ((app & APPLICATION_PGP) && (app & APPLICATION_SMIME))
4833  ts = _("PGP and S/MIME keys matching");

4837  ts = _("S/MIME keys matching");

4855 
4856  mutt_clear_error();
4857  k = NULL;

4859  {
4860  *forced_valid = 0;
4861  switch (mutt_menu_loop(menu))
4862  {
4863  case OP_VERIFY_KEY:
4864  verify_key(key_table[menu->current]);
4865  menu->redraw = REDRAW_FULL;
4866  break;
4867 
4868  case OP_VIEW_ID:
4869  mutt_message("%s", key_table[menu->current]->uid);
4870  break;
4871 
4872  case OP_GENERIC_SELECT_ENTRY:
4873  /* FIXME make error reporting more verbose - this should be
4874  * easy because GPGME provides more information */
4875  if (OptPgpCheckTrust)
4876  {
4877  if (!crypt_key_is_valid(key_table[menu->current]))
4878  {
4879  mutt_error(_("This key can't be used: "
4880  "expired/disabled/revoked"));
4881  break;
4882  }
4883  }
4884 
4885  if (OptPgpCheckTrust && (!crypt_id_is_valid(key_table[menu->current]) ||
4886  !crypt_id_is_strong(key_table[menu->current])))
4887  {
4888  const char *warn_s = NULL;
4889  char buf2[1024];
4890 
4891  if (key_table[menu->current]->flags & KEYFLAG_CANTUSE)
4892  {
4893  warn_s = _("ID is expired/disabled/revoked. Do you really want to "
4894  "use the key?");
4895  }
4896  else
4897  {
4898  warn_s = "??";
4899  switch (key_table[menu->current]->validity)
4900  {
4901  case GPGME_VALIDITY_NEVER:
4902  warn_s =
4903  _("ID is not valid. Do you really want to use the key?");
4904  break;
4905  case GPGME_VALIDITY_MARGINAL:
4906  warn_s = _("ID is only marginally valid. Do you really want to "
4907  "use the key?");
4908  break;
4909  case GPGME_VALIDITY_FULL:
4910  case GPGME_VALIDITY_ULTIMATE:
4911  break;
4912  case GPGME_VALIDITY_UNKNOWN:
4913  case GPGME_VALIDITY_UNDEFINED:
4914  warn_s = _("ID has undefined validity. Do you really want to "
4915  "use the key?");
4916  break;
4917  }
4918  }
4919 
4920  snprintf(buf2, sizeof(buf2), "%s", warn_s);
4921 
4922  if (mutt_yesorno(buf2, MUTT_NO) != MUTT_YES)
4923  {
4924  mutt_clear_error();
4925  break;
4926  }
4927 
4928  /* A '!' is appended to a key in find_keys() when forced_valid is
4929  * set. Prior to GPGME 1.11.0, encrypt_gpgme_object() called
4930  * create_recipient_set() which interpreted the '!' to mean set
4931  * GPGME_VALIDITY_FULL for the key.
4932  *
4933  * Starting in GPGME 1.11.0, we now use a '\n' delimited recipient
4934  * string, which is passed directly to the gpgme_op_encrypt_ext()
4935  * function. This allows to use the original meaning of '!' to
4936  * force a subkey use. */
4937 #if (GPGME_VERSION_NUMBER < 0x010b00) /* GPGME < 1.11.0 */
4938  *forced_valid = 1;
4939 #endif
4940  }
4941 
4942  k = crypt_copy_key(key_table[menu->current]);
4943  done = true;
4944  break;
4945 
4946  case OP_EXIT:
4947  k = NULL;
4948  done = true;
4949  break;
4950  }
4951  }
4952 
4953  mutt_menu_pop_current(menu);
4954  mutt_menu_free(&menu);
4955  FREE(&key_table);
4956  dialog_pop();
4957  mutt_window_free(&dlg);
4958 
4959  return k;

Parameters
[in]aAddress to match
[in]abilitiesAbilities to match, see KeyFlags

ptrMatching key

4998

5000

5005

5007  {
5008  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG5, " looking at key: %s '%.15s'\n", crypt_keyid(k), k->uid);
5009 
5010  if (abilities && !(k->flags & abilities))
5011  {
5012  mutt_debug(LL_DEBUG2, " insufficient abilities: Has %x, want %x\n", k->flags, abilities);
5013  continue;
5014  }
5015 
5016  this_key_has_strong = false; /* strong and valid match */
5017  this_key_has_addr_match = false;
5018  match = false; /* any match */
5019 
5020  struct AddressList alist = TAILQ_HEAD_INITIALIZER(alist);
5021  mutt_addrlist_parse(&alist, k->uid);
5022  struct Address *ka = NULL;
5023  TAILQ_FOREACH(ka, &alist, entries)
5024  {
5025  int validity = crypt_id_matches_addr(a, ka, k);
5026 
5027  if (validity & CRYPT_KV_MATCH) /* something matches */
5028  {
5029  match = true;
5030 
5031  if ((validity & CRYPT_KV_VALID) && (validity & CRYPT_KV_ADDR))
5032  {
5033  if (validity & CRYPT_KV_STRONGID)
5034  {
5035  if (the_strong_valid_key && (the_strong_valid_key->kobj != k->kobj))
5036  multi = true;
5037  this_key_has_strong = true;
5038  }
5039  else
5040  this_key_has_addr_match = true;
5041  }
5042  }
5043  }
5044  mutt_addrlist_clear(&alist);
5045 
5046  if (match)
5047  {
5048  struct CryptKeyInfo *tmp = crypt_copy_key(k);
5049  *matches_endp = tmp;
5050  matches_endp = &tmp->next;
5051 
5052  if (this_key_has_strong)
5053  the_strong_valid_key = tmp;
5054  else if (this_key_has_addr_match)
5055  a_valid_addrmatch_key = tmp;
5056  }
5057  }
5058 
5059  crypt_key_free(&keys);
5060 
5061  if (matches)
5062  {
5063  if (oppenc_mode)
5064  {
5065  if (the_strong_valid_key)
5066  k = crypt_copy_key(the_strong_valid_key);
5067  else if (a_valid_addrmatch_key && !C_CryptOpportunisticEncryptStrongKeys)
5068  k = crypt_copy_key(a_valid_addrmatch_key);
5069  else
5070  k = NULL;
5071  }
5072  else if (the_strong_valid_key && !multi)
5073  {
5074  /* There was precisely one strong match on a valid ID.
5075  * Proceed without asking the user. */
5076  k = crypt_copy_key(the_strong_valid_key);
5077  }
5078  else
5079  {
5080  /* Else: Ask the user. */
5081  k = crypt_select_key(matches, a, NULL, app, forced_valid);
5082  }
5083 
5084  crypt_key_free(&matches);
5085  }

5088 
5089  return k;
5090 }

static struct CryptKeyInfo* crypt_ask_for_key ( char *  tag,
char *  whatfor,
KeyFlags  abilities,
unsigned int  app,
int *  forced_valid 
)
static

Ask the user for a key.

Parameters
[in]tagPrompt to display
[in]whatforLabel to use (OPTIONAL)
[in]abilitiesFlags, see KeyFlags
[in]appApplication type, e.g. APPLICATION_PGP
[out]forced_validSet to true if user overrode key's validity
Return values
ptrCopy of the selected key

If whatfor is not null use it as default and store it under that label as the next default.

◆ find_keys()

static char* find_keys ( struct AddressList *  addrlist,
unsigned int  app,
bool  oppenc_mode 
)
static

Find keys of the recipients of the message.

Parameters
addrlistAddress List
appApplication type, e.g. APPLICATION_PGP
oppenc_modeIf true, use opportunistic encryption
Return values
ptrSpace-separated string of keys
NULLAt least one of the keys can't be found

If oppenc_mode is true, only keys that can be determined without prompting will be used.

5280  if (crypt_is_numerical_keyid(keyid))
5281  {
5282  if (strncmp(keyid, "0x", 2) == 0)
5283  keyid += 2;
5284  goto bypass_selection; /* you don't see this. */
5285  }
5286 
5287  /* check for e-mail address */
5288  mutt_addrlist_clear(&hookal);
5289  if (strchr(keyid, '@') && (mutt_addrlist_parse(&hookal, keyid) != 0))
5290  {
5291  mutt_addrlist_qualify(&hookal, fqdn);
5292  p = TAILQ_FIRST(&hookal);
5293  }
5294  else if (!oppenc_mode)
5295  {
5296  k_info = crypt_getkeybystr(keyid, KEYFLAG_CANENCRYPT, app, &forced_valid);
5297  }
5298  }
5299  else if (ans == MUTT_NO)
5300  {
5301  if (key_selected || STAILQ_NEXT(crypt_hook, entries))
5302  {
5303  crypt_hook = STAILQ_NEXT(crypt_hook, entries);
5304  continue;
5305  }
5306  }
5307  else if (ans == MUTT_ABORT)
5308  {
5309  FREE(&keylist);
5310  mutt_addrlist_clear(&hookal);
5311  mutt_list_free(&crypt_hook_list);
5312  return NULL;
5313  }
5314  }
5315 
5316  if (!k_info)
5317  {
5318  k_info = crypt_getkeybyaddr(p, KEYFLAG_CANENCRYPT, app, &forced_valid, oppenc_mode);
5319  }
5320 
5321  if (!k_info && !oppenc_mode)
5322  {
5323  snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), _("Enter keyID for %s: "), p->mailbox);
5324 
5325  k_info = crypt_ask_for_key(buf, p->mailbox, KEYFLAG_CANENCRYPT, app, &forced_valid);
5326  }
5327 
5328  if (!k_info)
5329  {
5330  FREE(&keylist);
5331  mutt_addrlist_clear(&hookal);
5332  mutt_list_free(&crypt_hook_list);
5333  return NULL;
5334  }
5335 
5336  keyid = crypt_fpr_or_lkeyid(k_info);
5337 
5338  bypass_selection:
5339  keylist_size += mutt_str_strlen(keyid) + 4 + 1;
5340  mutt_mem_realloc(&keylist, keylist_size);
5341  sprintf(keylist + keylist_used, "%s0x%s%s", keylist_used ? " " : "",
5342  keyid, forced_valid ? "!" : "");
5343  keylist_used = mutt_str_strlen(keylist);
5344 
5345  key_selected = true;
5346 
5347  crypt_key_free(&k_info);
5348  mutt_addrlist_clear(&hookal);
5349 
5350  if (crypt_hook)
5351  crypt_hook = STAILQ_NEXT(crypt_hook, entries);
5352 
5353  } while (crypt_hook);
5354 
5355  mutt_list_free(&crypt_hook_list);
5356  }
5357  return keylist;
5358 }

◆ mutt_gpgme_select_secret_key()

int mutt_gpgme_select_secret_key ( struct Buffer *  keyid)

Select a private Autocrypt key for a new account.

Parameters
keyidAutocrypt Key id
Return values
0Success
-1Error

Unfortunately, the internal ncrypt/crypt_gpgme.c functions use CryptKeyInfo, and so aren't exportable.

This function queries all private keys, provides the crypt_select_keys() menu, and returns the selected key fingerprint in keyid.

Definition at line 5389 of file crypt_gpgme.c.

5390 {
5391  int rc = -1, junk;
5392  gpgme_error_t err;
5393  gpgme_key_t key = NULL;
5394  gpgme_user_id_t uid = NULL;
5395  struct CryptKeyInfo *results = NULL, *k = NULL;
5396  struct CryptKeyInfo **kend = NULL;
5397  struct CryptKeyInfo *choice = NULL;
5398 
5399  gpgme_ctx_t ctx = create_gpgme_context(false);
5400 
5401  /* list all secret keys */
5402  if (gpgme_op_keylist_start(ctx, NULL, 1))
5403  goto cleanup;
5404 
5405  kend = &results;
5406 
5407  while (!(err = gpgme_op_keylist_next(ctx, &key)))
5408  {
5409  KeyFlags flags = KEYFLAG_NO_FLAGS;
5410 
5411  if (key_check_cap(key, KEY_CAP_CAN_ENCRYPT))
5412  flags |= KEYFLAG_CANENCRYPT;
5413  if (key_check_cap(key, KEY_CAP_CAN_SIGN))
5414  flags |= KEYFLAG_CANSIGN;
5415 
5416  if (key->revoked)
5417  flags |= KEYFLAG_REVOKED;
5418  if (key->expired)
5419  flags |= KEYFLAG_EXPIRED;
5420  if (key->disabled)
5421  flags |= KEYFLAG_DISABLED;
5422 
5423  int idx;
5424  for (idx = 0, uid = key->uids; uid; idx++, uid = uid->next)
5425  {
5426  k = mutt_mem_calloc(1, sizeof(*k));
5427  k->kobj = key;
5428  gpgme_key_ref(k->kobj);
5429  k->idx = idx;
5430  k->uid = uid->uid;
5431  k->flags = flags;
5432  if (uid->revoked)
5433  k->flags |= KEYFLAG_REVOKED;
5434  k->validity = uid->validity;
5435  *kend = k;
5436  kend = &k->next;
5437  }
5438  gpgme_key_unref(key);
5439  }
5440  if (gpg_err_code(err) != GPG_ERR_EOF)
5441  mutt_error(_("gpgme_op_keylist_next failed: %s"), gpgme_strerror(err));
5442  gpgme_op_keylist_end(ctx);
5443 
5444  if (!results)
5445  {
5446  /* L10N: mutt_gpgme_select_secret_key() tries to list all secret keys to choose
5447  from. This error is displayed if no results were found. */
5448  mutt_error(_("No secret keys found"));
5449  goto cleanup;
5450  }
5451 
5452  choice = crypt_select_key(results, NULL, "*", APPLICATION_PGP, &junk);
5453  if (!(choice && choice->kobj && choice->kobj->subkeys && choice->kobj->subkeys->fpr))
5454  goto cleanup;
5455  mutt_buffer_strcpy(keyid, choice->kobj->subkeys->fpr);
5456 
5457  rc = 0;
5458 
5459 cleanup:
5460  crypt_key_free(&choice);
5461  crypt_key_free(&results);
5462  gpgme_release(ctx);
5463  return rc;
5464 }

5599 {

5620  prompt =
5621  _("S/MIME (s)ign, sign (a)s, (p)gp, (c)lear, or (o)ppenc mode off?");
5622  /* L10N: S/MIME options (opportunistic encryption is on) */
5623  letters = _("sapco");
5624  choices = "SapCo";
5625  }
5626  else
5627  {
5628  /* L10N: PGP options (opportunistic encryption is on) */
5629  prompt =
5630  _("PGP (s)ign, sign (a)s, s/(m)ime, (c)lear, or (o)ppenc mode off?");
5631  /* L10N: PGP options (opportunistic encryption is on) */
5632  letters = _("samco");
5633  choices = "SamCo";
5634  }
5635  }
5636  /* Opportunistic encryption option is set, but is toggled off for this message. */
5637  else if (C_CryptOpportunisticEncrypt)
5638  {
5639  if (is_smime)
5640  {
5641  /* L10N: S/MIME options (opportunistic encryption is off) */
5642  prompt = _("S/MIME (e)ncrypt, (s)ign, sign (a)s, (b)oth, (p)gp, (c)lear, "
5643  "or (o)ppenc mode?");
5644  /* L10N: S/MIME options (opportunistic encryption is off) */
5645  letters = _("esabpco");
5646  choices = "esabpcO";
5647  }
5648  else
5649  {
5650  /* L10N: PGP options (opportunistic encryption is off) */
5651  prompt = _("PGP (e)ncrypt, (s)ign, sign (a)s, (b)oth, s/(m)ime, (c)lear, "
5652  "or (o)ppenc mode?");
5653  /* L10N: PGP options (opportunistic encryption is off) */
5654  letters = _("esabmco");
5655  choices = "esabmcO";
5656  }
5657  }
5658  /* Opportunistic encryption is unset */
5659  else
5660  {
5661  if (is_smime)
5662  {
5663  /* L10N: S/MIME options */
5664  prompt =
5665  _("S/MIME (e)ncrypt, (s)ign, sign (a)s, (b)oth, (p)gp or (c)lear?");
5666  /* L10N: S/MIME options */
5667  letters = _("esabpc");
5668  choices = "esabpc";
5669  }
5670  else
5671  {
5672  /* L10N: PGP options */
5673  prompt =
5674  _("PGP (e)ncrypt, (s)ign, sign (a)s, (b)oth, s/(m)ime or (c)lear?");
5675  /* L10N: PGP options */
5676  letters = _("esabmc");
5677  choices = "esabmc";
5678  }
5679  }
5680 
5681  choice = mutt_multi_choice(prompt, letters);
5682  if (choice > 0)
5683  {
5684  switch (choices[choice - 1])
5685  {
5686  case 'a': /* sign (a)s */
5687  p = crypt_ask_for_key(_("Sign as: "), NULL, KEYFLAG_CANSIGN,
5688  is_smime ? APPLICATION_SMIME : APPLICATION_PGP, NULL);
5689  if (p)
5690  {
5691  char input_signas[128];
5692  snprintf(input_signas, sizeof(input_signas), "0x%s", crypt_fpr_or_lkeyid(p));
5693  mutt_str_replace(is_smime ? &C_SmimeDefaultKey : &C_PgpSignAs, input_signas);
5694  crypt_key_free(&p);
5695 
5696  e->security |= SEC_SIGN;
5697  }
5698  break;
5699 
5700  case 'b': /* (b)oth */
5701  e->security |= (SEC_ENCRYPT | SEC_SIGN);
5702  break;
5703 
5704  case 'C':
5705  e->security &= ~SEC_SIGN;
5706  break;
5707 
5708  case 'c': /* (c)lear */
5709  e->security &= ~(SEC_ENCRYPT | SEC_SIGN);
5710  break;
5711 
5712  case 'e': /* (e)ncrypt */
5713  e->security |= SEC_ENCRYPT;
5714  e->security &= ~SEC_SIGN;
5715  break;
5716 
5717  case 'm': /* (p)gp or s/(m)ime */
5718  case 'p':
5719  is_smime = !is_smime;
5720  if (is_smime)
5721  {
5722  e->security &= ~APPLICATION_PGP;
5723  e->security |= APPLICATION_SMIME;
5724  }
5725  else
5726  {
5727  e->security &= ~APPLICATION_SMIME;
5728  e->security |= APPLICATION_PGP;
5729  }
5730  crypt_opportunistic_encrypt(e);
5731  break;
5732 
5733  case 'O': /* oppenc mode on */
5734  e->security |= SEC_OPPENCRYPT;
5735  crypt_opportunistic_encrypt(e);
5736  break;
5737 
5738  case 'o': /* oppenc mode off */
5739  e->security &= ~SEC_OPPENCRYPT;
5740  break;
5741 
5742  case 'S': /* (s)ign in oppenc mode */
5743  e->security |= SEC_SIGN;
5744  break;
5745 
5746  case 's': /* (s)ign */
5747  e->security &= ~SEC_ENCRYPT;
5748  e->security |= SEC_SIGN;
5749  break;
5750  }
5751  }
5752 
5753  return e->security;
5754 }

5800  for (uid = key->uids; uid && rc; uid = uid->next)
5801  {
5802  int uid_length = strlen(uid->email);
5803  if ((uid->email[0] == '<') && (uid->email[uid_length - 1] == '>') &&
5804  (uid_length == (sender_length + 2)))
5805  {
5806  const char *at_sign = strchr(uid->email + 1, '@');
5807  if (at_sign)
5808  {
5809  /* Assume address is 'mailbox@domainname'.
5810  * The mailbox part is case-sensitive,
5811  * the domainname is not. (RFC2821) */
5812  const char *tmp_email = uid->email + 1;
5813  const char *tmp_sender = sender->mailbox;
5814  /* length of mailbox part including '@' */
5815  int mailbox_length = at_sign - tmp_email + 1;
5816  int domainname_length = sender_length - mailbox_length;
5817  int mailbox_match, domainname_match;
5818 
5819  mailbox_match = (strncmp(tmp_email, tmp_sender, mailbox_length) == 0);
5820  tmp_email += mailbox_length;
5821  tmp_sender += mailbox_length;
5822  domainname_match =
5823  (strncasecmp(tmp_email, tmp_sender, domainname_length) == 0);
5824  if (mailbox_match && domainname_match)
5825  rc = false;
5826  }
5827  else
5828  {
5829  if (strncmp(uid->email + 1, sender->mailbox, sender_length) == 0)
5830  rc = false;
5831  }
5832  }
5833  }
5834  }
5835  else
5836  mutt_any_key_to_continue(_("Failed to verify sender"));
5837  }
5838  else
5839  mutt_any_key_to_continue(_("Failed to figure out sender"));
5840

5842  {
